title: dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. francis-drucei
intro: Dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. francis-drucei, also known as Francis-Druce's marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the United Kingdom and Ireland. It is a type of orchid that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. francis-drucei is often used in herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with medications.
